purpose: Stylesheet for the Phase 6 launcher page. Establishes the yellow-on-black Aperture aesthetic, centers the picker in the viewport, lays out the 4 chamber buttons in a 2x2 grid (collapses to a single column under 40em), applies hover/focus inversion, and animates the blinking footer cursor. Styles two stacked feature panels: (1) ENRICHMENT CENTER CREDITS — bordered section header + help text, monospace textarea with focus border, char-counter and #f80 truncation notice meta row, MODE fieldset with custom-accent radios; (2) OVERRIDE TRACK — same bordered header treatment, button row, status meta row with audio-status / audio-error / WE-disabled pill spans, and a .disabled state that dims the entire section under ?we=1. The .hidden utility class is the launcher.js toggle contract for the MODE fieldset, truncation notice, audio status, audio error, and WE pill. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.
invariants: Background #000 / foreground #ff0 (pure yellow) matches the splash overlay aesthetic in portal/, portal2/, and portal2/portal1style/.
Hover/focus state inverts to background #ff0 / foreground #000; .subtitle also re-tints to #330 so it remains legible against the yellow.
font-family: monospace, 12pt body — matches the credits typography in the variants for visual continuity.
#cursor uses ::before { content: '_' } so the blinking character is CSS-driven and can be styled without HTML changes.
.hidden { display: none !important } is the launcher.js toggle contract for the MODE fieldset, truncation notice, audio status, audio error pill, and WE pill.
Both .enrichment and .override-track use the same border treatment (1px solid #ff0, identical padding) so the two feature panels read as a matched pair.
.override-track.disabled dims border + text to #550 (dark yellow-brown) — distinct from .hidden which removes the element entirely. The disabled state is for visible-but-unusable (WE mode).
gotchas: Grid collapses to a single column at 40em viewport width; the header letter-spacing is also reduced to keep it on one line on narrow viewports.
Without prefers-reduced-motion the cursor blinks via steps(1) keyframes (hard on/off, not smooth fade) and buttons have an 80ms color transition; both are disabled when prefers-reduced-motion is set.

color #cc0 (sub-yellow) is used for subheaders, subtitles, footer text, char-counter, and audio-status; #f80 (amber) is reserved for truncation notice, audio-error, and the WE pill border so they pop against the yellow palette.
Radio buttons use accent-color #ff0 which Chrome/Firefox honor but older browsers may ignore — they will still function, just with default accent color.
.we-pill uses an amber border + amber text — visually distinct from the other meta-row spans so the 'feature disabled' state reads as intentional rather than as an error.

The .disabled class only restyles colors — it does NOT add pointer-events:none or opacity. launcher.js sets the disabled attribute on the buttons separately so clicks no-op even though the cursor doesn't change.
